assertions.expectation: Explains that multi-host TPU slices are recreated atomically in a single step — not a rolling upgrade
assertions.reason: Response explains that TPU slices will be drained and recreated on new physical hardware, and notes that all 4 slices may not be maintained simultaneously but losing even one slice stops training, implying atomic recreation

assertions.expectation: Warns that a maintenance event on ANY single slice triggers restart of ALL slices in the multislice environment
assertions.reason: Response explicitly states 'All 4 slices may not be maintained simultaneously, but losing even one slice stops training' and 'multislice training requires all slices to be healthy'

assertions.expectation: Notes that all pods on all affected slices will restart simultaneously — the training job will be interrupted
assertions.reason: Response clearly states 'Training will halt completely' and 'All in-memory state will be lost', indicating simultaneous interruption

assertions.expectation: Explains that if insufficient TPU resources are available during recreation, the slice blocks until hardware is freed — potentially causing extended downtime
assertions.reason: Response mentions 'TPU v5e-256 availability can be limited' and 'Your slices might not come back immediately' in the post-maintenance section

assertions.expectation: Recommends saving a training checkpoint immediately before the maintenance window
assertions.reason: Response includes 'Force a checkpoint save before the maintenance window' in the pre-maintenance checklist

assertions.expectation: Recommends configuring the training framework for automatic checkpoint resume so it can restart after the maintenance event
assertions.reason: Response shows checkpoint configuration code and includes restart policies in YAML, plus mentions 'Load from latest checkpoint' in recovery steps

assertions.expectation: Suggests using maintenance exclusions to defer the event if the training run is close to completion
assertions.reason: Response mentions 'Contact Google Cloud Support to reschedule if critical' under Option B: Reschedule Maintenance

assertions.expectation: Mentions maintenance windows to control when the event occurs — schedule it during a planned break in training
assertions.reason: Response mentions 'Maintenance usually happens during your region's maintenance window' and suggests Option A: Controlled Restart to 'Stop training gracefully before maintenance'
